description:
Five code changes to align implementation with the updated Gist.BranchingStrategies.md:

1. Swap A↔B behavior: Strategy A (stay-on-main) should write directly to Dolt main with no branch created. Strategy B (merge-with-branch) should be isolated until explicit merge. Strategy C unchanged.
2. Replace single-char strategy values (A/B/C) with slugs (stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close) everywhere: schema CHAR(1)→VARCHAR(32), CLI flags, env var, prompts, display, store validation.
3. Rewrite dolt_head.go: replace .beads/DOLT_HEAD with .beads/HEAD (branch pointer) + .beads/refs/heads/<branch> (commit hash). Update hooks.go read path and vc.go writeDoltHead call.
4. Strategy C conflict resolution: remove --theirs auto-resolution, instead log warning and skip merge (retry on next close). Keep --theirs for Strategy B where branch is authoritative during active work.
5. Add dormant status alongside abandoned in UnregisterBranch validation. Four states: active, dormant, merged, abandoned.

Files: branch.go, branch_strategy.go, branch_messaging.go, checkout.go, dolt_head.go, hooks.go, vc.go, store.go, 009_beads_branches.go

Update Gist.BranchingStrategies.md to reflect implementation decisions and ensure bidirectional consistency:

1. Section 4.7 conflict resolution: clarify that --theirs auto-resolution applies to Strategy B (branch is authoritative during active work) but Strategy C skips on conflict and retries on next close. Current text only describes the skip behavior without distinguishing B vs C.
2. Section 5.3 branch registry: add dormant and abandoned as explicit status values (currently only mentions active, dormant, merged). Add abandoned for branches discarded without merge.
3. Section 8.5 interactive prompt: update prompt text to use slug names (stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close) with A/B/C as shortcuts, matching the updated code UX.
4. Section 4.1: clarify that Strategy A does NOT create a Dolt branch at all — RegisterBranch skips branch creation for stay-on-main.

Phase 1: .beads/HEAD + refs (independent, low risk, no behavioral change)
Phase 2: Strategies A (stay-on-main) + B (merge-with-branch) — B is a no-op on post-commit

description: Epic tracking implementation of branch isolation features per Gist.BranchingStrategies.md. Three complementary features extending existing Beads infrastructure with no new daemon: (1) Per-branch merge strategy (stay-on-main / merge-with-branch / merge-on-close), (2) Cross-branch message delivery via cherry-pick, (3) BEADS_HEAD pointer file for git-dolt correspondence, (4) Protected branch merge via CI. All features are Beads-level. Target: v0.58.0+.
design:
## Data Model

### beads_branches registry table (migration 009)
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS beads_branches (
    branch_name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY,
    merge_strategy VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'stay-on-main',
    status VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
)
```
merge_strategy: stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close. status: active, merged, abandoned. main is never registered.

### Strategy naming
- Canonical names (config, flags, env vars, DB): stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close
- Interactive shortcuts (prompt only): A, B, C
- Section labels (docs): Strategy A, Strategy B, Strategy C

### .beads/DOLT_HEAD pointer file
Format: dolt_commit: <hash> + dolt_branch: <branch>. Written after every Dolt auto-commit. Git-tracked.

### Configuration
- branch.prompt_strategy: true/false (default true) — prompt on new branch
- branch.default_strategy: stay-on-main/merge-with-branch/merge-on-close (default stay-on-main)
- BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var: same values as config
- Resolution order: --strategy flag > BD_MERGE_STRATEGY > branch.default_strategy > stay-on-main

### Implementation Order (by phase)
Phase 1: bd-vlu.1.1 (BEADS_HEAD) → bd-vlu.1.2 (post-checkout hook)
Phase 2: bd-vlu.2.1 (registry) → bd-vlu.2.2 (strategy selection) → bd-vlu.2.3 (Strategy A) + bd-vlu.2.4 (Strategy B) → bd-vlu.2.5 (merge CI flags) → bd-vlu.2.6 (naming alignment)
Phase 3: bd-vlu.3.1 (Strategy C) → bd-vlu.3.2 (conflict resolution)
Phase 4: bd-vlu.4.1 (cross-branch messaging) → bd-vlu.4.2 (schema migration) + bd-vlu.4.3 (GitHub Action)

### Key Decisions
- Second *sql.DB connection for merge operations (Strategy C) in Dolt server mode
- Registry table on main, not per-branch
- Cherry-pick for messaging (not merge — preserves isolation)
- BEADS_HEAD is a simple text file, not JSON
- Strategy A (stay-on-main) has no Dolt branch — writes directly to main
- Declarative strategy names in all programmatic interfaces; A/B/C only for interactive prompt
title: Dolt Branch Isolation: Per-Branch Merge Strategies, Cross-Branch Messaging, and Git-Dolt History Correspondence

description:
Pointer files (.beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/<branch>), post-checkout hook synchronization, and writeBeadsRefs integration into commit paths. Purely additive — records Dolt state into git-tracked files. No existing behavior changes.

Status: Functionally complete (bd-vlu.1.1 and bd-vlu.1.2 closed). Remaining work is bd-vlu.1.3 — manual validation test in ~/Projects/math covering ref writes, time-travel reset, rebase/stash safety, and BD_AUTO_SYNC=1.

Gate: Refs write correctly on every Dolt commit, read properly during git checkout, sync prompts function in interactive and BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 modes, rebase/stash safety confirmed.
title: Phase 1: Beads Refs

close_reason: Created dolt_head.go with writeDoltHead/readDoltHead functions. Hooked writeDoltHead into both transact() and maybeAutoCommit() paths. File writes dolt_commit and dolt_branch to .beads/DOLT_HEAD and git-adds it.
description:
Implement git-Dolt correspondence pointer files (gist §6). Mirror git's own ref structure:

- `.beads/HEAD` — contains `ref: refs/heads/<branch>` identifying current Dolt branch
- `.beads/refs/heads/<branch>` — contains Dolt commit hash at branch tip

After every Dolt auto-commit:
1. Write current branch name to .beads/HEAD (if changed)
2. Write DOLT_HASHOF('HEAD') to .beads/refs/heads/<current-branch>
3. git add .beads/HEAD .beads/refs/heads/<current-branch>

Read path (§6.4): post-checkout git hook reads .beads/HEAD to determine Dolt branch, compares hash in .beads/refs/heads/<branch> against current Dolt state. On mismatch, prompt: [R]eset Beads DB / [K]eep current. Reset uses DOLT_RESET('--hard', hash). Agent mode: BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 auto-selects Reset. Rebase/stash safety (§6.5): skip during rebase-in-progress, skip file-level checkouts.
title: Implement Beads refs (.beads/HEAD + .beads/refs/heads/)

close_reason: Enhanced runPostCheckoutHook with BEADS_HEAD sync logic and branch strategy prompting. Added postCheckoutBeadsSync, maybePromptBranchStrategy, isTerminal helpers. Added ResetToCommit method to DoltStore. Supports BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 for non-interactive mode, BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var for auto-registration. Skip logic for file-level checkouts and rebase-in-progress.
description:
Create/extend post-checkout git hook (gist §4.4, §6.4, §6.5). Two responsibilities:

(1) Beads ref sync: On branch switch, read .beads/HEAD to determine expected Dolt branch, compare .beads/refs/heads/<branch> hash against current Dolt state. On mismatch, prompt Reset/Keep. Safety guards: skip during rebase (check rebase-merge/rebase-apply dirs), skip during stash (check third arg=0), skip file-level checkouts.

(2) Branch strategy prompt: On new branch creation, detect unregistered Dolt branch, prompt for merge strategy [A/B/C], register in beads_branches.

Non-interactive support via BD_MERGE_STRATEGY and BD_AUTO_SYNC env vars.
title: Add post-checkout git hook for Beads ref sync and branch strategy prompt

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math as test bed.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads initialized with Dolt server running
- Post-checkout hook installed (bd hooks install)

## Test 1: Refs written on Dolt commits
1. cd ~/Projects/math && git checkout main
2. bd create --title="Refs test issue 1" --type=task
3. Verify .beads/HEAD contains "ref: refs/heads/main"
4. Verify .beads/refs/heads/main contains a Dolt commit hash (32+ char hex string)
5. Record the hash: REF1=$(cat .beads/refs/heads/main)
6. bd update <id> --status=in_progress
7. Verify .beads/refs/heads/main hash changed (new Dolt commit)
8. git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint 1"

## Test 2: Time-travel reset prompt
1. bd create --title="Refs test issue 2" --type=task
2. git add -A && git commit -m "checkpoint 2"
3. git checkout HEAD~1
4. Observe: post-checkout hook should detect mismatch and prompt [R]eset / [K]eep
5. Choose R (Reset) — verify bd list shows only issue 1 (issue 2 not yet created at checkpoint 1)
6. git checkout main — verify bd list shows both issues again

## Test 3: Rebase safety
1. git checkout -b test/rebase-safety
2. bd create --title="Rebase test" --type=task
3. git add -A && git commit -m "rebase test commit"
4. git rebase main — verify hook does NOT fire on intermediate checkouts (no prompt)
5. git checkout main && git branch -d test/rebase-safety

## Test 4: Stash safety
1. echo "temp" >> sums/1.txt
2. git stash
3. Verify: no Beads ref sync prompt fires
4. git stash pop

## Test 5: BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 (non-interactive)
1. git checkout HEAD~1
2. BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 git checkout HEAD~1 — verify auto-reset with no prompt
3. git checkout main

## Definition of Done
- [ ] .beads/HEAD always contains correct branch ref after Dolt commits
- [ ] .beads/refs/heads/<branch> hash updates on every Dolt commit
- [ ] Post-checkout hook detects mismatch and prompts correctly
- [ ] Reset restores Dolt state matching the checked-out git commit
- [ ] No spurious prompts during rebase or stash operations
- [ ] BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 works without TTY
title: [Test] Validate Beads refs and time-travel reset

description:
Add Go tests for the beads refs feature (Phase 1). Currently zero automated test coverage — all validation was manual.

## Summary

Automated tests for writeBeadsRefs(), readBeadsRefs(), and the postCheckoutBeadsSync() control flow. These are the core functions enabling git-Dolt time-travel correspondence. Tests should be unit-level where possible (no Dolt server needed for ref file I/O) and use existing test patterns (newTestStoreWithPrefix, t.TempDir()).

## Functional Requirements

1. **Ref file I/O** — writeBeadsRefs creates correct file structure; readBeadsRefs round-trips correctly
2. **Edge cases** — missing files, malformed HEAD content, missing refs dir, empty hash
3. **Hook control flow** — runPostCheckoutHook skips on flag!=1, skips during rebase, calls sync on flag=1
4. **Mismatch detection and reset correctness** — postCheckoutBeadsSync detects hash mismatch and triggers DOLT_RESET. This is the highest-value, highest-risk test. DOLT_RESET('--hard', hash) resets ALL Dolt-managed tables to the state at that commit. The test MUST verify:
   - **Issues created AFTER the checkpoint are gone** — they must not appear in query results after reset
   - **Issues that existed AT the checkpoint are preserved** — they must still be present with correct fields (title, status, priority, type, description)
   - **Dependencies created after checkpoint are gone** — dependency rows added after the checkpoint must not survive reset
   - **Labels created after checkpoint are gone** — label rows added after the checkpoint must not survive reset
   - **Comments created after checkpoint are gone** — comment rows added after the checkpoint must not survive reset
   - **Pre-checkpoint dependencies/labels/comments are preserved** — all auxiliary data from before the checkpoint must survive intact
   - **Round-trip integrity** — after resetting backward then forward (simulating checkout old → checkout new), ALL data reappears exactly as before with no corruption or duplication
   - **Ref files match after reset** — .beads/refs/heads/<branch> contains the hash we reset TO, not the hash we reset FROM
   - Test uses BD_AUTO_SYNC=1 code path (non-interactive)
5. **No math references** — grep guard ensuring no ~/Projects/math or math-* issue IDs leaked into the codebase

## Technical Direction

- File: cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go (ref I/O tests, no DB needed)
- File: cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go (hook control flow, needs mock or test store for sync tests)
- Pattern: match existing test style (//go:build cgo, package main, newTestStoreWithPrefix)
- readBeadsRefs/writeBeadsRefs are pure I/O — testable with t.TempDir() alone
- postCheckoutBeadsSync needs a real DoltStore for the reset path; use newTestStoreWithPrefix
- isRebaseInProgress testable by creating/removing .git/rebase-merge sentinel dirs
design:
## Test Plan

### Unit Tests (cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go) — no Dolt needed

**readBeadsRefs**:
- Happy path: valid HEAD + valid ref → returns (hash, branch)
- Missing HEAD file → ("", "")
- Malformed HEAD (no "ref: refs/heads/" prefix) → ("", "")
- HEAD valid but ref file missing → ("", branch)
- Trailing whitespace/newlines in files → trimmed correctly
- Branch name with slashes (e.g., "feature/foo") → handled

**writeBeadsRefs round-trip**:
- Write then read → same hash and branch returned
- Creates refs/heads/ directory if missing
- Overwrites existing ref file on update
- Nil or closed store → returns without error (no-op)

### Unit Tests (cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go) — no Dolt needed

**runPostCheckoutHook arg filtering**:
- args with flag=1 → calls postCheckoutBeadsSync (via side effect detection)
- args with flag=0 → returns 0 without sync
- args too short (<3) → returns 0 without sync

**isRebaseInProgress**:
- No sentinel dirs → false
- .git/rebase-merge exists → true
- .git/rebase-apply exists → true
- Both exist → true

### Integration Tests (cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go) — needs Dolt

**TestPostCheckoutResetCorrectness** (highest-value test):
Setup:
1. Create store, create issue A with label, dependency, and comment → Dolt auto-commit → record hash as CHECKPOINT
2. Create issue B with its own label, dependency, and comment → Dolt auto-commit → record hash as LATEST
3. Write .beads/refs with CHECKPOINT hash (simulating git checkout to older commit)

Reset backward (LATEST → CHECKPOINT):
4. Set BD_AUTO_SYNC=1, call postCheckoutBeadsSync()
5. Assert issue B is GONE (query returns not-found)
6. Assert issue A is PRESENT with correct title, status, priority, type, description
7. Assert issue A's label, dependency, and comment are PRESENT
8. Assert issue B's label, dependency, and comment are GONE
9. Assert .beads/refs/heads/<branch> contains CHECKPOINT hash

Reset forward (CHECKPOINT → LATEST):
10. Write .beads/refs with LATEST hash, call postCheckoutBeadsSync() again
11. Assert BOTH issues A and B are present with all original fields intact
12. Assert ALL labels, dependencies, and comments for both issues are present
13. Assert no duplicate rows (count matches expected)
14. Assert .beads/refs/heads/<branch> contains LATEST hash

**TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncInSync**:
- Write refs matching current Dolt hash → no reset occurs, Dolt HEAD unchanged

**TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncNoRefs**:
- No ref files present → skip gracefully (no panic, no reset)

### Static Guard (cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go)

**No math test bed references**:
- Grep all .go files for "math-" issue ID patterns (math-ryv, math-r62, etc.)
- Grep all .go files for "Projects/math" path references
- Test fails if any matches found (excluding test file itself)

### Run command
go test -v -run TestBeadsRefs ./cmd/bd/
go test -v -run TestPostCheckout ./cmd/bd/
go test -v -run TestNoMathReferences ./cmd/bd/
notes:
## Technical Architecture

**Scope**: Backend only (Go tests in cmd/bd package)

**Components in scope**:
- cmd/bd/dolt_head.go: writeBeadsRefs(), readBeadsRefs() — pure filesystem I/O
- cmd/bd/hooks.go: runPostCheckoutHook() control flow, postCheckoutBeadsSync() mismatch detection, isRebaseInProgress()
- Codebase-level grep guard for math references

**Components out of scope**:
- DoltStore.ResetToCommit() and GetCurrentCommit() — already tested in internal/storage/dolt/versioned_test.go
- Interactive TTY prompt path in postCheckoutBeadsSync() — difficult to test in CI, covered by manual test

**Test tiers**:
1. Pure unit tests (no DB): readBeadsRefs, writeBeadsRefs round-trip, isRebaseInProgress, runPostCheckoutHook arg filtering — use t.TempDir()
2. Integration tests (needs Dolt test container): postCheckoutBeadsSync mismatch → reset flow — use newTestStoreWithPrefix()
3. Static guard: grep test ensuring no math-* or ~/Projects/math references in committed code

**Patterns to follow**:
- Build tag: //go:build cgo (matches existing tests)
- Test helper: newTestStoreWithPrefix(t, path, prefix) for Dolt-backed tests
- t.TempDir() for filesystem-only tests
- enableTestModeGlobals() for tests that touch package globals

## Implementation Plan

1. **Create cmd/bd/dolt_head_test.go**
   - //go:build cgo, package main
   - TestReadBeadsRefs: 6 subtests (happy path, missing HEAD, malformed HEAD, missing ref, whitespace, slashed branch)
   - TestWriteReadBeadsRefsRoundTrip: write with mock store data, read back, verify equality
   - TestWriteBeadsRefsNilStore: verify no panic on nil store
   - TestNoMathReferences: filepath.WalkDir on repo root, scan .go files for "math-" and "Projects/math"

2. **Create cmd/bd/hooks_checkout_test.go**
   - //go:build cgo, package main
   - TestRunPostCheckoutHookArgFiltering: 3 subtests (flag=1, flag=0, short args)
   - TestIsRebaseInProgress: 3 subtests using t.TempDir() with sentinel dirs
   - TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncMismatch: integration test using newTestStoreWithPrefix — write refs with stale hash, set BD_AUTO_SYNC=1, call postCheckoutBeadsSync, verify Dolt HEAD matches saved hash
   - TestPostCheckoutBeadsSyncInSync: same setup but matching hashes → verify no reset

3. **Verify**: go test -v -run "TestReadBeadsRefs|TestWriteRead|TestNoMath|TestRunPostCheckout|TestIsRebase|TestPostCheckoutBeadsSync" ./cmd/bd/
title: [Test] Automated tests for beads refs and time-travel sync

description:
Registry table (beads_branches), bd branch command, bd checkout command, strategy selection via flags/environment variables, extended bd vc merge with CI flags.

Strategy A (stay-on-main): direct writes to main, no Dolt branch created.
Strategy B (merge-with-branch): full isolation, explicit merge via bd vc merge.

Status: Implementation complete (bd-vlu.2.1 through bd-vlu.2.6 all closed). Remaining work is 6 test beads (bd-vlu.2.7 through bd-vlu.2.12) — manual validation in ~/Projects/math covering Strategy A, Strategy B, registry lifecycle, CI merge workflow, mixed strategies, and retry-after-failure.

Note: bd-vlu.2.11 (mixed strategies) and bd-vlu.2.12 (retry) are integration tests that depend on Phase 1 refs and Phase 3/4 features.

Gate: Branch creation and registration function correctly; Strategy A preserves existing behavior; Strategy B isolation works; CI merge workflow operational.
title: Phase 2: Strategies A + B

description:
Create the beads_branches table on Dolt main (gist §5.3). Schema:

  branch_name VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY
  merge_strategy VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'stay-on-main'
  status VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
  updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

merge_strategy: stay-on-main, merge-with-branch, merge-on-close. status: active, dormant, merged, abandoned. main is never registered. This is the single source of truth for active branches. Add schema migration (009). Registration lifecycle: INSERT on branch creation, UPDATE status on deletion/pause. SELECT active branches for cherry-pick targeting.
title: Create beads_branches registry table and migration

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server

## Test 1: CI merge workflow
1. git checkout -b test/ci-merge
2. bd branch test/ci-merge --strategy merge-with-branch
3. bd create --title="CI merge test issue" --type=task
4. echo "11 + 0 = 11" > differences/11.txt && git add -A && git commit -m "add differences/11.txt"
5. bd dolt push (if federation remote configured)
6. Simulate CI: bd vc merge --from=test/ci-merge --to=main --cleanup
7. Verify: bd list on main shows the issue
8. Verify: bd dolt sql "SELECT status FROM beads_branches WHERE branch_name='test/ci-merge'" returns 'merged'
9. Verify: .beads/refs/heads/main hash updated after merge

## Test 2: Failure path (non-zero exit on conflict)
1. git checkout -b test/ci-conflict
2. bd branch test/ci-conflict --strategy merge-with-branch
3. Manufacture a conflict (create issue, modify same field from main)
4. bd vc merge --from=test/ci-conflict --to=main — verify non-zero exit code
5. Verify: test/ci-conflict branch still exists (--cleanup only runs on success)

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -d test/ci-merge test/ci-conflict

## Definition of Done
- [ ] bd vc merge --from/--to/--cleanup works for CI workflow
- [ ] --cleanup deletes source branch and marks 'merged' in registry
- [ ] .beads/refs/heads/main updated after merge
- [ ] Non-zero exit on conflicts
- [ ] --cleanup does not run on failed merge
title: [Test] Validate bd vc merge CI workflow with --from/--to/--cleanup

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math. Integration test spanning all strategies.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server
- All three strategies implemented (depends on Phase 3 Strategy C + Phase 4 messaging)

## Test 1: Create mixed-strategy branches
1. git checkout -b polecat-alpha && bd branch polecat-alpha --strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout main && git checkout -b polecat-beta && bd branch polecat-beta --strategy stay-on-main
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b polecat-gamma && bd branch polecat-gamma --strategy merge-on-close

## Test 2: Create issues on each branch
1. git checkout polecat-alpha && bd create --title="Alpha issue (B)" --type=task
2. git checkout polecat-beta && bd create --title="Beta issue (A)" --type=task
3. git checkout polecat-gamma && bd create --title="Gamma issue (C)" --type=task

## Test 3: Verify main visibility
1. git checkout main && bd list
2. Verify: Beta issue IS visible (Strategy A — writes to main)
3. Verify: Alpha issue is NOT visible (Strategy B — isolated)
4. Verify: Gamma issue is NOT visible (Strategy C — WIP isolated)

## Test 4: Close gamma issue
1. git checkout polecat-gamma
2. bd close <gamma-id> --reason="test close"
3. git checkout main && bd list
4. Verify: Gamma issue now IS visible (merge-on-close triggered)

## Test 5: Merge alpha branch
1. bd vc merge --from=polecat-alpha --to=main
2. bd list
3. Verify: Alpha issue now visible on main

## Test 6: Cross-branch messages (requires Phase 4)
1. git checkout polecat-beta
2. bd create --title="Message from beta" --type=message
3. git checkout polecat-alpha && bd list --type=message
4. Verify: message arrived via cherry-pick
5. git checkout polecat-gamma && bd list --type=message
6. Verify: message arrived via cherry-pick

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D polecat-alpha polecat-beta polecat-gamma

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Strategy A: immediate visibility on main
- [ ] Strategy B: invisible until explicit merge
- [ ] Strategy C: invisible until bd close
- [ ] Messages delivered across all strategies
- [ ] beads_branches registry accurate throughout
title: [Test] Validate mixed-strategy scenario — polecats on different strategies

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math. Tests retry-after-failure workflow from gist §1.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running, post-checkout hook installed

## Test 1: Create branch and do work
1. git checkout -b attempt-1
2. bd branch attempt-1 --strategy merge-with-branch
3. bd create --title="First approach" --type=task --priority=1
4. echo "11 + 0 = 11" > sums/11.txt
5. echo "12 + 0 = 12" > sums/12.txt
6. git add -A && git commit -m "attempt 1 work"
7. bd update <id> --status=in_progress --notes="Working on first approach"

## Test 2: Simulate failure and reset
1. Record current state: git log --oneline -3
2. git checkout main
3. Observe: Beads ref sync prompt fires (Dolt branch vs main mismatch)
4. Choose Reset — Dolt switches back to main
5. git branch -D attempt-1

## Test 3: Verify clean state
1. bd list — verify attempt-1 issues NOT visible on main (Strategy B isolation)
2. ls sums/ — verify sums/11.txt and sums/12.txt gone (git reset)

## Test 4: Retry with new approach
1. git checkout -b attempt-2
2. bd branch attempt-2 --strategy merge-with-branch
3. bd create --title="Second approach (better)" --type=task --priority=1
4. echo "11 + 1 = 12" > sums/11.txt
5. git add -A && git commit -m "attempt 2 work"
6. bd vc merge --from=attempt-2 --to=main --cleanup
7. git checkout main && bd list
8. Verify: only attempt-2 issue visible, no leftover from attempt-1

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Strategy B branch can be abandoned cleanly
- [ ] No orphaned Dolt state from deleted branches
- [ ] New branch starts clean with no contamination from previous attempt
- [ ] Merge brings only the successful attempt to main
title: [Test] End-to-end retry-after-failure workflow

description: Add Go tests for Phase 2 branch strategy functionality: strategy registration, stay-on-main (A) behavior, merge-with-branch (B) isolation, beads_branches registry table schema (VARCHAR(32) merge_strategy, status lifecycle). Also verify no code in the beads repo accidentally references the math test bed (math-* issue IDs, ~/Projects/math paths).
title: [Test] Automated tests for branch strategies A + B

description: Document Phase 2 changes in changelog: beads_branches registry table, strategy A (stay-on-main) behavior, strategy B (merge-with-branch) isolation, branch registration prompt on first checkout.
title: Update changelog for Phase 2: Strategies A + B

close_reason: Updated bd branch command: --strategy flag (A/B/C), BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var support, default A. Branch creation now uses RegisterBranch which creates Dolt branch + registry entry. List view shows strategy and status.
description: Implement strategy selection UX (§5.4, §9). bd branch command with --strategy flag accepting stay-on-main|merge-with-branch|merge-on-close. resolveStrategy function checks flag, BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var, branch.default_strategy config, then defaults to stay-on-main. strategyDisplayName maps canonical names to display. Register choice in beads_branches table.
title: Implement per-branch merge strategy selection and storage

close_reason: Strategy B auto-merge is implemented in branch_strategy.go. executePostCommitStrategy calls autoMergeToMain for strategy B branches after every Dolt commit. Uses dedicated merge connection (separate sql.DB) that checks out main, merges branch, and commits. Conflict handling: log warning, skip, retry on next commit.
description: Implement stay-on-main mode (§5.1). Git branches independently of Dolt — worker stays on Dolt main, all bd writes go directly to main. No Dolt branch created, no merge step needed. The simplest strategy. Only difference from having no strategy: branch is registered in beads_branches for message routing. Use cases: real-time visibility, Mayor tracking convoy progress, iterative work.
title: Implement Strategy A — Stay on main (real-time)

close_reason: Strategy A is the default (no auto-merge). Created checkout.go for bd checkout command, branch_strategy.go with executePostCommitStrategy engine, autoMergeToMain helper using dedicated merge connection. Hooked strategy execution into both transact and maybeAutoCommit paths. Added CommitAuthor() and ConnectionString() accessors to DoltStore.
description: Implement fully isolated Dolt branch mode (§5.2). Issue state reaches main only when git branch merges (via Refinery or manual merge). On branch creation with strategy merge-with-branch: create Dolt branch from main. All bd writes target the Dolt branch. No auto-merge to main. Dolt merge happens when bd vc merge is called (manually or by CI). Use cases: bake-offs, retry-after-failure, speculative work.
title: Implement Strategy B — Merge with branch (isolated)

close_reason: Extended bd vc merge with --from, --to, --cleanup flags. CI workflow: bd vc merge --from=feature --to=main --cleanup. Non-zero exit on unresolved conflicts. Auto-commits merge, updates BEADS_HEAD, and optionally cleans up source branch (deletes Dolt branch + marks 'merged' in registry).
description: Extend existing bd vc merge command (gist §7.3) for CI protected branch workflows. New flags: --from=<branch> and --to=<branch> for explicit source/target (overrides positional arg and current branch). --cleanup: deletes source Dolt branch after successful merge and marks it 'merged' in beads_branches. Exit non-zero on merge conflict for CI failure. CI workflow: bd dolt pull → bd vc merge --from=$HEAD_REF --to=main --cleanup → bd dolt push. Also updates .beads/HEAD and .beads/refs/heads/main after merge.
title: Extend bd vc merge with CI flags (--from, --to, --cleanup)

close_reason: Superseded by bd-anc which implemented the strategy naming alignment (slugs, A/B/C shortcuts, refs format)
description:
The design doc was revised to reorder strategies (A=stay-on-main, B=merge-with-branch, C=merge-on-close) and use declarative names instead of single letters. The existing code implementation uses the old labels (A=isolated, B=auto-merge). This task updates the code to match.

## Changes required

### 1. Swap A↔B labels in code
Files: branch_strategy.go, hooks.go, branch.go, close.go, dolt_autocommit.go

### 2. Use declarative names instead of single letters
- beads_branches.merge_strategy: VARCHAR(32) storing stay-on-main|merge-with-branch|merge-on-close (migration 010)
- --strategy flag: accept declarative names
- BD_MERGE_STRATEGY env var: accept declarative names
- Interactive prompt: keep A/B/C shortcuts, map to declarative names

### 3. Add config keys
- branch.prompt_strategy (bool, default true)
- branch.default_strategy (string, default stay-on-main)

### 4. Strategy A simplification
- Strategy A (stay-on-main) should NOT create a Dolt branch
- Only Strategy B and C create Dolt branches

### Reference
See Gist.BranchingStrategies.md §5, §9 for full spec.
title: Align code with revised strategy naming: A↔B swap + declarative names + config keys

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server

## Test 1: Strategy A basic flow
1. git checkout -b test/strategy-a
2. bd branch test/strategy-a --strategy stay-on-main
3. bd create --title="Strategy A test issue" --type=task --priority=2
4. Verify: bd list shows the issue
5. git checkout main
6. Verify: bd list STILL shows the issue (writes went directly to Dolt main)

## Test 2: No Dolt branch created
1. git checkout test/strategy-a
2. bd branch — verify listing shows "stay-on-main [active]" for test/strategy-a
3. Run: bd dolt sql "SELECT * FROM dolt_branches" — verify NO Dolt branch named test/strategy-a exists

## Test 3: Concurrent Strategy A branches
1. git checkout -b test/strategy-a-2
2. bd branch test/strategy-a-2 --strategy stay-on-main
3. bd create --title="Second Strategy A issue" --type=task
4. git checkout main
5. Verify: bd list shows BOTH issues from both branches

## Test 4: Registry verification
1. bd dolt sql "SELECT * FROM beads_branches WHERE branch_name LIKE 'test/strategy-a%'"
2. Verify: both branches registered with merge_strategy='stay-on-main', status='active'

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -d test/strategy-a test/strategy-a-2

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Strategy A writes go directly to Dolt main — no isolation
- [ ] No Dolt branch created for Strategy A
- [ ] Branch registered in beads_branches with correct strategy
- [ ] Multiple concurrent Strategy A branches work correctly
title: [Test] Validate Strategy A stay-on-main — issue updates visible on main immediately

description:
Merge-on-close behavior in bd close, dedicated merge connection, skip-and-retry conflict handling. Introduces auto-merge path — the first piece writing to main from non-main context. Isolated validation of the dedicated connection pattern.

Status: Implementation complete (bd-vlu.3.1 and bd-vlu.3.2 closed). Remaining work is 2 test beads (bd-vlu.3.3 and bd-vlu.3.4) — manual validation in ~/Projects/math covering merge-on-close visibility and conflict resolution.

Gate: Close operations trigger merges to main; conflicts handled gracefully with skip-and-retry; dedicated connection does not interfere with worker sessions; contention negligible at typical scale.
title: Phase 3: Strategy C

close_reason: Strategy C merge-on-close implemented. Added maybeStrategyC_MergeOnClose to branch_strategy.go. Hooked into close.go after CloseIssue call. When current branch has strategy C, auto-merges to main via dedicated connection after each issue close.
description: Implement auto-merge to main only on bd close / bd update --status=closed (§5.3). Same mechanism as Strategy B (§5.5) but auto-merge triggers only on issue close, not every write. Reduces contention on main to one merge per issue lifecycle. Middle ground between A and B. Use case: most polecat workflows — focused isolation during work, automatic visibility on completion.
title: Implement Strategy C — Merge on close (lifecycle-gated)

close_reason: Enhanced autoMergeToMain with retry logic (3 attempts with exponential backoff) and conflict auto-resolution using DOLT_CONFLICTS_RESOLVE --theirs (branch changes win). Resolves conflicts across issues, dependencies, labels, comments, events tables. Retries on serialization delays and connection errors.
description: Implement conflict resolution and retry logic (§5.7) for Strategy C auto-merges. Strategy A (stay-on-main) needs no merge. Strategy B (merge-with-branch) defers to explicit bd vc merge. Strategy C (merge-on-close) auto-merge on bd close may encounter conflicts — retry with theirs strategy, exponential backoff for serialization delay.
title: Add conflict resolution and retry logic for Strategy C auto-merges

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server

## Test 1: Non-conflicting concurrent updates (cell-level merge)
1. git checkout -b test/conflict-a && bd branch test/conflict-a --strategy merge-on-close
2. git checkout main && git checkout -b test/conflict-b && bd branch test/conflict-b --strategy merge-on-close
3. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Conflict test issue" --type=task --priority=1
4. bd close <id> --reason="merge to main" (triggers auto-merge)
5. From test/conflict-b: bd update <id> --notes="Adding notes from branch B"
6. bd close <id-of-another-issue> --reason="merge to main"
7. Verify: both updates on main (cell-level merge, no conflict — different fields)

## Test 2: Conflicting updates (same cell)
1. From test/conflict-a: bd create --title="Same cell conflict" --type=task --priority=0
2. bd close <id> --reason="merge" (triggers auto-merge, gets to main)
3. From test/conflict-b: bd update <same-id> --priority=3
4. bd close <another-id> --reason="trigger merge"
5. Observe: merge conflict on priority field — should see warning in stderr
6. Verify: branch B's close still succeeds (work continues), merge skipped with warning

## Test 3: Self-healing retry
1. From test/conflict-b: make another bd update (any field)
2. bd close <yet-another-id> --reason="retry merge"
3. Observe: retry attempt — does it merge cleanly now?

## Cleanup
git checkout main && git branch -D test/conflict-a test/conflict-b

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Non-conflicting concurrent updates merge cleanly (cell-level)
- [ ] True conflicts (same cell) abort gracefully with warning
- [ ] Work continues on branch after failed merge
- [ ] Retry on next close attempts merge again
- [ ] Warning message clear enough for debugging
title: [Test] Validate conflict resolution — manufacture and resolve merge conflicts

description: Add Go tests for Phase 3 cross-branch messaging: cherry-pick delivery of message-type issues to all active branches, message deduplication, delivery tracking. Also verify no code in the beads repo accidentally references the math test bed (math-* issue IDs, ~/Projects/math paths).

description: Document Phase 3 changes in changelog: message-type issues, cherry-pick delivery to active branches, message deduplication and delivery tracking.
title: Update changelog for Phase 3: Cross-Branch Messaging

description:
Cherry-pick delivery for messages, schema migration coordination, dormant branch status handling. Highest-complexity piece — requires the branch registry (Phase 2) and merge connection pattern (Phase 3).

Status: Core messaging implemented (bd-vlu.4.1 closed). Two implementation beads remain open: bd-vlu.4.2 (schema migration safety) and bd-vlu.4.3 (GitHub Action template). Four test beads (bd-vlu.4.4 through bd-vlu.4.7) provide manual validation in ~/Projects/math.

Gate: Messages reach all active branches; cherry-pick failures log gracefully; schema migrations coordinate properly; dormant branches skip delivery and catch up on resume.
title: Phase 4: Cross-Branch Messaging

close_reason: Created branch_messaging.go with deliverMessageToActiveBranches, cherryPickToTarget, and maybeSendMessage. Uses DOLT_CHERRY_PICK via dedicated delivery connection. Hooks into create.go after post-create commit. Cherry-pick failures log warning and continue (no dirty state). On conflict, DOLT_MERGE --abort cleans up.
description: Implement message cherry-pick mechanism (§6.1-6.6). When bd create -t message or message update occurs: (1) Commit message on current branch, capture commit hash. (2) Cherry-pick to all active branches via dedicated delivery connection — iterate beads_branches WHERE status='active' AND branch_name \!= current. Messages are append-only inserts with hash-based IDs, should apply cleanly. On cherry-pick failure: log warning, continue (clean failure, no dirty state). Dolt commit message records source branch. Handle concurrency (§6.5): O(N) cherry-picks per message, ~50-100ms at 3-5 polecats.
title: Implement cross-branch message delivery via cherry-pick

description: Handle schema migration coordination across branches (§6.4.1). DOLT_CHERRY_PICK doesn't support schema-change commits. Implementation: (1) Add migration suspension flag in beads_branches or config table. (2) During migration: suspend message delivery, run migration on all active branches (iterate beads_branches WHERE status='active'), resume delivery. (3) Update bd migrate to handle multi-branch migration loop. Schema migrations are rare (version 6 across project lifetime) so this is a safety mechanism.
title: Implement schema migration safety for cross-branch cherry-picks

description: Create .github/workflows/beads-merge.yml template (§8.2). Triggers on pull_request closed+merged to main. Steps: checkout, install beads-cli, bd dolt pull, bd vc merge --from=HEAD_REF --to=main --cleanup, bd dolt push. Requires federation remote configured. Optional: second Action on PR open/update generates Dolt diff as PR comment (§8.4) via bd dolt diff --from=main --to=branch --format=markdown showing issue state changes.
title: Create GitHub Action workflow template for Beads merge on PR

description:
Manual test plan using ~/Projects/math. Tests agent bake-off workflow from gist §1.

## Prerequisites
- cd ~/Projects/math
- Beads running with Dolt server, messaging enabled

## Test 1: Set up competing branches
1. git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-1 && bd branch bakeoff/agent-1 --strategy merge-with-branch
2. git checkout main && git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-2 && bd branch bakeoff/agent-2 --strategy merge-with-branch
3. git checkout main && git checkout -b bakeoff/agent-3 && bd branch bakeoff/agent-3 --strategy merge-with-branch

## Test 2: Each agent does independent work
1. git checkout bakeoff/agent-1
2. bd create --title="Agent 1 approach: sequential sums" --type=task
3. mkdir -p products && echo "sequential approach" > products/README.md
4. git add -A && git commit -m "agent 1 work"
5. Repeat for agent-2 and agent-3 with different approaches

## Test 3: Pick winner and merge
1. git checkout main
2. bd vc merge --from=bakeoff/agent-2 --to=main --cleanup
3. git merge bakeoff/agent-2 (git-side merge)
4. bd list — verify ONLY agent-2 issues visible on main

## Test 4: Discard losers
1. git branch -D bakeoff/agent-1 bakeoff/agent-3
2. bd list — verify NO ghost issues from agent-1 or agent-3
3. bd dolt sql "SELECT branch_name, status FROM beads_branches WHERE branch_name LIKE 'bakeoff/%'"
4. Verify: agent-2 status='merged', agent-1 and agent-3 need manual cleanup or remain 'active' (document which)

## Test 5: Messages preserved
1. Before discarding, send messages from bakeoff branches
2. After merge, verify messages from all branches preserved on main (cherry-picked during bake-off)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Winner's issues reach main via merge
- [ ] Loser branches leave no issue state on main
- [ ] Messages sent during bake-off preserved on main
- [ ] Clean workflow for discarding losing branches
- [ ] beads_branches registry reflects final state
title: [Test] End-to-end agent bake-off workflow

title: [Test] Automated tests for merge-on-close strategy C

description: Document Phase 4 changes in changelog: strategy C lifecycle-gated merging, conflict resolution, branch status transitions (active/dormant/merged/abandoned).
title: Update changelog for Phase 4: Merge-on-Close (Strategy C)
